title: Hero/preview + best-path mock scores 'Scholarly articles: Met' from the word 'conference' — a talk read as a publication
expected: A conference TALK or a KubeCon keynote must NOT light up 'Scholarly articles' as Met. Scholarly = papers/journals/citations; my talks are press/recognition, not authorship. Nothing I didn't claim should read 'Met' (acceptance criterion #2, #5).
got: The deterministic mock (`mockQualification`) scores each criterion by `pc.match.test(text)`. The SCHOLARLY regex includes `conference`: `/\b(paper|publication|published|journal|conference|citation|cited|scholar|arxiv)\b/i`. My pasted record says 'KubeCon keynote' and 'two conference talks', so 'conference' trips SCHOLARLY → status 'Met', evidence 'Mentions publications, papers, or citations.' This is the hero InstantVerdict's read (my FIRST impression), the `/qualify` best-path ranking, and every keyless/CI build. It is exactly the 'talk mis-categorized as a publication' pet peeve, hallucinated by a keyword.
suggested_acceptance: Either drop `conference` from the SCHOLARLY keyword heuristic (conference talks are not scholarly authorship), or in the mock cap a `conference`-only hit at 'Partial' for SCHOLARLY so a talk never reads 'Met' as a publication. The authenticated model path already isolates this via Rule 4; the fix is to stop the keyless preview from over-scoring scholarly on a talk.
l2_priority: Paste Kenji's exact OSS+keynote record into BOTH the hero InstantVerdict and the authenticated /qualify (real Claude). Confirm: (a) the hero does not show 'Scholarly articles: Met' off the talks; (b) the real model marks Scholarly thin/None and never paraphrases a talk into a publication.

title: Best-path ranking is mock/keyword-only — it can recommend the wrong route for an OSS record the keyword heuristic mis-reads
expected: When I land on /qualify and use 'Find my best path' across O-1A/O-1B/EB-1A, the recommendation should be reasoned over my real record, not a coin-flip (qualify-verdict definition-of-done).
got: `scoreAllPrograms` → `mockQualification` per pack with `source: 'mock'` — the ranker NEVER calls the model. The recommendation is derived from the same keyword heuristic that mis-scores Scholarly (kw-qualify-01) and leans on the ORIGINAL regex catching `github|open-source|stars|downloads`. For an OSS record this is fragile: if a keyword misfires, the ranking that picks O-1A vs EB-1A inherits the error. The single-classification authenticated screen (`/api/qualify`) IS model-backed; only the cross-program comparison is mock.
suggested_acceptance: Documented in the module as a deliberate keyless baseline ('a model-backed multi-pack scoring is a future extension', best-path.ts:13-14). Acceptable as a comparison heuristic IF the UI frames it as a keyword pre-read (it does not, unlike the hero's SoftGate). Either label the best-path result as a quick keyword read, or back the recommendation with the model once the user is authenticated.
l2_priority: Run best-path on Kenji's record; confirm the recommended program is sane for an O-1A OSS self-petitioner and that the keyword mis-scores don't flip the recommendation to EB-1A/O-1B.

title: Draft argues from per-criterion evidence/rationale, not my full pasted CV — my star/download/adopter specifics survive only if the qualify model captured them
expected: The draft's Original-contribution and Critical-role sections cite MY actual adoption facts (38k stars, the Helm pulls, Datadog/Shopify/named banks, the KubeCon keynote) the way a strong drafter would — not generic 'extraordinary ability' filler.
got: The draft prompt is fed ONLY `petitioner`, `classification`, and the scored criteria rendered as `- name [status]: evidence — rationale` (criterion-text.ts:35-40), plus any vault exhibit facts. There is NO persisted `profile`/CV column (petitions.ts has no profile field; createCaseWithCriteria stores petitioner/classification/likelihood/criteria only). So the draft can only name what the qualify model already paraphrased into each criterion's `evidence`/`rationale` (cap 4000 chars each, generous) or what I later add to the Evidence Vault. A terse qualify capture = a thinner draft; my raw paste never reaches the draft prompt.
suggested_acceptance: This is the ACCEPTED/RESOLVED backlog item PN-DRAFT-01 (G1.3): decided 2026-06-19 no code change — L2 proved the live draft already names supplied specifics because the qualify model captures them into the criteria evidence/rationale, and the 'populate the vault first' nudge (DraftStudio.tsx:378-387) covers enrichment. Recorded here only to confirm the SAME boundary holds for an OSS record (where the specifics are stars/adopters, not papers). Not a new finding.
l2_priority: THE ballgame: paste Kenji's OSS record into /qualify (real Claude), open the case, draft. Assert the Original-contribution + Critical-role section bodies name his real project, star/download numbers, named adopters, and keynote — not generic filler — and that Scholarly is argued honestly as thin (talks ≠ papers).

title: STRENGTH: live adjudication flags invented numbers/companies and UPL drift on every paid draft
expected: If the model fabricates a star count, a download number, or a company I never named, I want it surfaced before my attorney wastes time on it.
got: Every paid draft runs `runAdjudication`: `fabricationGate` warns on any $/%/year/integer≥100 in the output not traceable to the input (an invented '50k stars' or a fake adopter number is flagged 'verify'); `classificationGate` fails on a leaked wrong visa code; `caseLawGate` warns on any case-law cite; `disclaimerGate` fails if the UPL disclaimer is missing/altered. The CITATION_RULE + `auditCitations` quarantine any (Exhibit N) that doesn't resolve to a real on-file document. This is precisely the anti-hallucination posture an OSS self-petitioner needs.
suggested_acceptance: Protect this. Do not weaken fabricationGate or the citation audit. (Note for L2: fabrication is a 'warn', not a hard block — correct posture, but verify the warn is visibly surfaced in DraftStudio's AdjudicationBadge so I actually see it.)
l2_priority: Confirm the AdjudicationBadge renders the fabrication/case-law warnings visibly on a real draft, and that a deliberately-injected fake metric trips the warn.

title: Keyless categorize mock can drop an OSS adoption doc into 'Scholarly articles' on the same 'conference' keyword collision
expected: A 'KubeCon 2025 keynote' or a 'conference talk recording' document should bucket into Press/recognition or Judging context — never silently into 'Scholarly articles' as if it were a paper (organize-evidence: no silent wrong-bucketing).
got: `mockCategorize` picks the FIRST pack criterion whose `match` tests true over `name + content`. The criteria are tested in pack order; SCHOLARLY (index 5) shares the `conference` keyword. A doc named/described with 'conference' or 'keynote' can land in 'Scholarly articles' if an earlier criterion doesn't match first. Same root cause as kw-qualify-01. Mitigated for the AUTHENTICATED path: when LLM_ENGINE=claude the real model categorizes (and now sees the existing-vault summary, G2.1), so this is a keyless-build / CI defect, not the live local path.
suggested_acceptance: Same fix as kw-qualify-01: removing `conference` from the SCHOLARLY heuristic fixes both the qualify mock and the categorize mock in one edit. Refile-to-correct-bucket is available as a manual override, and the honest 'documents present ≠ criterion proven' framing already ships (EvidenceVault.tsx:210-216), so the blast radius is bounded.
l2_priority: With LLM_ENGINE=claude, add a KubeCon-keynote doc and a star-history doc to the vault; confirm the real model buckets them into recognition/original-contribution, not Scholarly, and extracts accurate facts (no invented numbers).

title: STRENGTH: vault categorizes into MY O-1A pack, sees siblings for consistency, and frames coverage honestly
expected: Evidence sorts into the correct O-1A buckets (my pack, not a researcher template), coverage is real, and the tool doesn't pretend 'document present' means 'criterion proven'.
got: Categorize is fed the document's full `content`, the case `classification` (so it uses MY eight O-1A buckets via `packFor`), AND a read-only summary of what's already filed (`summarizeVaultBuckets`, G2.1/PN-EVID-01 shipped) so a new doc is placed consistently with siblings. `summarizeVault` derives coverage/gaps from the real pack, exhibit numbers are monotonic in the store, and the UI states plainly that evidence-on-file is not proof and refile is a manual move (dc-evidence-02 addressed).
suggested_acceptance: Protect. The classification-scoped buckets + whole-vault consistency + honest coverage framing are exactly right for an OSS record.

title: Dashboard always renders the mock 'Dr. Anya Krishnan · 92% approval' researcher masthead next to my real case
expected: When I open /dashboard with my own real case, the page should center MY case. A terse engineer who distrusts theater shouldn't see a fabricated researcher persona with a hardcoded '92% approval likelihood' presented as a live case file alongside his own.
got: `CaseFileDashboard` shows my real cases in `YourCasesCard` at the TOP (good), but then unconditionally renders the demo masthead 'Dr. Anya Krishnan / O1-241 / Senior Research Engineer / O-1A', an 'Open petition letter' button, a 'Voice intake transcript' button, and badges including 'Status: Drafting' + '92% approval likelihood' — illustrative mock data, not mine. For me specifically (no papers, distrusts horoscope framing) this researcher persona and the fake 92% read as the tool defaulting me into the researcher mold I came here to escape.
suggested_acceptance: This is the ACCEPTED 'Mock demo case file' gap (accepted-gaps.md:24-25 — illustrative mock shown alongside real cases, not editable). Recorded as a clarity nit from Kenji's distrust-of-theater lens, NOT a new defect: the real-case affordance (YourCasesCard → /dashboard/cases/[id]) is present and correct. If revisited later, gate the demo masthead off once the user has a real case, or label it 'Sample case'.
l2_priority: Confirm a non-attorney user with one real case lands on a dashboard where their case is unmistakably theirs and the demo is clearly a sample (or absent).

title: STRENGTH: roadmap + deep-link + empty-state derive purely from real case state
expected: I always know my stage and what's next; deep links from qualify hydrate the case; an empty dashboard points me to /qualify.
got: `caseRoadmap(status, {hasEvidence, hasDraft})` is pure and status-driven; `CaseDetailView` feeds it `documents.length>0` and the real draft sections, so the stepper reflects what I actually did. The empty state routes to /qualify (CaseFileDashboard.tsx:112-133); qualify's 'Open case file' deep-links to /dashboard/cases/[id] hydrated from saved state (QualifyPanel.tsx:284-291; case page loads criteria/draft/docs/events). No dead-ends.
suggested_acceptance: Protect the pure roadmap derivation and the deep-link hydration.

title: STRENGTH: share token leaks nothing private, renders DB-free, validates against the pack, and frames honestly
expected: I can send my counsel a credible verdict link without exposing my pasted record, and it must not overclaim a legal grant.
got: `encodeSnapshot` puts ONLY name/classification/likelihood/per-criterion-status into a base64url token — never the profile text (letters-patent.ts:21-29, 69-77). `decodeSnapshot` rejects a tampered token whose status count doesn't match the live pack, so the coat-of-arms can't drift. /c/[token] renders from the token alone (no DB), stamps 'Qualifies' only at/above threshold and 'In progress' otherwise, and carries 'Informational only · not legal advice · no account needed'. The OG image route shares the same codec.
suggested_acceptance: Protect. Privacy-by-construction (no DB, no profile in token) and the honest informational framing are exactly right.
l2_priority: Mint a share link from Kenji's result; confirm the URL carries no profile text and the card reads credible (not cheesy) for sending to immigration counsel.

title: A share minted from the hero/keyword read can encode a mock-inflated 'Scholarly: Met' into the certificate I send my attorney
expected: If I share a 'Letters Patent' from the hero before signing in, the statuses on it should be honest — a talk should not appear as a Met scholarly criterion on a card I forward to counsel.
got: `LettersPatentShare` is mounted in BOTH the hero `InstantVerdict` (mock result) and the authenticated `QualifyPanel` (real model). The shared statuses come straight from `result.criteria`. If I share from the hero, the snapshot inherits the keyword mock's over-scored 'Scholarly articles: Met' (kw-qualify-01), so the certificate I forward to my immigration counsel can show a criterion I don't actually meet. The card itself is honest about being informational, but the per-criterion statuses can be wrong at the source.
suggested_acceptance: Fixing kw-qualify-01 (the SCHOLARLY/conference heuristic) removes the inflated status at the source, which is the cleanest fix. Alternatively, the share affordance could note the hero card is a keyword pre-read until re-screened by the model — but the heuristic fix is preferred.
l2_priority: Mint a share from the HERO read on Kenji's record and decode the token; confirm no criterion he doesn't meet (esp. Scholarly) shows as Met on the certificate.
